name: kubectl
context: Use kubectl to interact with Kubernetes clusters — apply manifests, inspect resources, debug workloads, and manage config contexts
info: kubectl is the primary CLI for Kubernetes. Every cluster operation — deploying apps, inspecting Pods, reading logs, scaling Deployments, port-forwarding, managing contexts — goes through kubectl. It translates commands into Kubernetes API calls against the active cluster context defined in KUBECONFIG. Agents use kubectl to apply YAML manifests, query resource state with JSON/YAML output for parsing, run imperative commands for quick operations, and pipe output to other tools. This skill covers the complete kubectl command vocabulary: resource CRUD (get, describe, create, apply, delete, patch), debugging (logs, exec, debug, port-forward, cp), rollouts (rollout status, undo, restart), scaling, config context management, and output formatting. See [[kubernetes/pods]] for Pod-specific operations, [[kubernetes/deployments]] for rollout workflows, and [[kubernetes/troubleshooting]] for systematic debugging.
body:
# kubectl

kubectl is the command-line interface for Kubernetes. Every interaction with a cluster — deploying workloads, reading logs, debugging failures, managing configuration — flows through kubectl or the equivalent API calls.

## Installation

```bash
# macOS
brew install kubectl

# Linux (latest stable)
curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/release/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl"
chmod +x kubectl && sudo mv kubectl /usr/local/bin/

# Verify
kubectl version --client
```

## Context and Configuration

kubectl uses a kubeconfig file (default `~/.kube/config`) to know which cluster, user, and namespace to target.

```bash
# List available contexts
kubectl config get-contexts

# Switch context
kubectl config use-context production-cluster

# Set default namespace for current context
kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=my-app

# View current context
kubectl config current-context

# View full config
kubectl config view --minify

# Merge multiple kubeconfigs
export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config:~/.kube/staging-config
kubectl config view --flatten > ~/.kube/merged-config
```

## Core Verbs

| Verb | Purpose | Example |
|------|---------|----------|
| `get` | List resources | `kubectl get pods -o wide` |
| `describe` | Detailed info + events | `kubectl describe pod my-app` |
| `apply` | Create/update from manifest | `kubectl apply -f deploy.yaml` |
| `create` | Imperative create | `kubectl create deployment nginx --image=nginx` |
| `delete` | Remove resources | `kubectl delete -f deploy.yaml` |
| `patch` | Partial update | `kubectl patch deploy my-app -p '{"spec":{"replicas":5}}'` |
| `edit` | Open in $EDITOR | `kubectl edit deployment my-app` |
| `logs` | Container stdout/stderr | `kubectl logs my-pod --tail=100` |
| `exec` | Run command in container | `kubectl exec -it my-pod -- /bin/sh` |
| `port-forward` | Local tunnel to pod/svc | `kubectl port-forward svc/my-app 8080:80` |
| `scale` | Change replica count | `kubectl scale deploy my-app --replicas=5` |
| `rollout` | Manage rollouts | `kubectl rollout status deploy/my-app` |
| `debug` | Ephemeral debug container | `kubectl debug pod/my-app -it --image=busybox` |
| `cp` | Copy files to/from pod | `kubectl cp my-pod:/app/log.txt ./log.txt` |
| `top` | Resource usage (needs metrics-server) | `kubectl top pods` |

## Output Formatting

```bash
# Wide output (node, IP)
kubectl get pods -o wide

# JSON output (for parsing)
kubectl get pod my-app -o json

# YAML output
kubectl get pod my-app -o yaml

# JSONPath — extract specific fields
kubectl get pod my-app -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}'
kubectl get pods -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'
kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.status.conditions[-1].type}{"\n"}{end}'

# Custom columns
kubectl get pods -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.phase,NODE:.spec.nodeName

# Sort by field
kubectl get pods --sort-by=.status.startTime
kubectl get events --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
```

## Filtering and Selection

```bash
# By namespace
kubectl get pods -n production
kubectl get pods --all-namespaces  # or -A

# By label
kubectl get pods -l app=my-app
kubectl get pods -l 'app in (web, api)'
kubectl get pods -l 'version!=v1'

# By field
kubectl get pods --field-selector status.phase=Running
kubectl get pods --field-selector spec.nodeName=node-1
kubectl get events --field-selector type=Warning

# Watch for changes
kubectl get pods --watch
kubectl get events -w --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
```

## Dry Run and Manifest Generation

Generate manifests imperatively without creating resources:

```bash
# Generate Deployment YAML
kubectl create deployment web-app --image=nginx:1.25 --replicas=3 \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > deployment.yaml

# Generate Service YAML
kubectl expose deployment web-app --port=80 --target-port=8080 \
  --type=ClusterIP --dry-run=client -o yaml > service.yaml

# Generate Job YAML
kubectl create job data-import --image=myapp:latest \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > job.yaml

# Generate ConfigMap from literals
kubectl create configmap app-config \
  --from-literal=DB_HOST=postgres \
  --from-literal=LOG_LEVEL=info \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > configmap.yaml

# Generate Secret
kubectl create secret generic db-creds \
  --from-literal=password=s3cret \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > secret.yaml

# Server-side diff before applying
kubectl diff -f deployment.yaml
```

## Apply and Manage Manifests

```bash
# Apply a single file
kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml

# Apply all files in a directory
kubectl apply -f ./k8s/

# Apply from URL
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/org/repo/main/deploy.yaml

# Apply with kustomize
kubectl apply -k ./overlays/production/

# Prune resources not in manifests
kubectl apply -f ./k8s/ --prune -l app=my-app

# Delete resources from manifest
kubectl delete -f deployment.yaml

# Force replace (dangerous — causes downtime)
kubectl replace --force -f deployment.yaml
```

## Debugging Commands

```bash
# Logs from a specific container
kubectl logs my-pod -c sidecar --tail=100 -f

# Previous container logs (after crash)
kubectl logs my-pod --previous

# Exec into container
kubectl exec -it my-pod -- /bin/sh
kubectl exec my-pod -- env  # non-interactive

# Ephemeral debug container (k8s 1.23+)
kubectl debug pod/my-pod -it --image=busybox --target=app

# Port-forward to pod or service
kubectl port-forward pod/my-pod 8080:8080
kubectl port-forward svc/my-service 3000:80

# Copy files
kubectl cp my-pod:/var/log/app.log ./app.log
kubectl cp ./fix.conf my-pod:/etc/app/fix.conf

# Run a one-off debug pod
kubectl run debug --rm -it --image=nicolaka/netshoot -- /bin/bash
kubectl run curl-test --rm -it --image=curlimages/curl -- curl -s http://my-service
```

## Resource Management

```bash
# Scale a deployment
kubectl scale deployment my-app --replicas=5

# Autoscale (create HPA)
kubectl autoscale deployment my-app --min=2 --max=10 --cpu-percent=80

# Rollout operations
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-app
kubectl rollout history deployment/my-app
kubectl rollout undo deployment/my-app
kubectl rollout restart deployment/my-app

# Label resources
kubectl label pod my-pod env=production
kubectl label pod my-pod env-  # remove label

# Annotate resources
kubectl annotate deployment my-app 'deploy.note=hotfix for #123'

# Taint nodes
kubectl taint nodes node-1 dedicated=gpu:NoSchedule
kubectl taint nodes node-1 dedicated-  # remove taint

# Cordon/uncordon nodes (prevent/allow scheduling)
kubectl cordon node-1
kubectl uncordon node-1

# Drain node (evict pods before maintenance)
kubectl drain node-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
```

## Useful Aliases

```bash
# Add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc
alias k='kubectl'
alias kgp='kubectl get pods'
alias kgs='kubectl get svc'
alias kgd='kubectl get deployments'
alias kd='kubectl describe'
alias kl='kubectl logs'
alias ka='kubectl apply -f'
alias kx='kubectl exec -it'
alias kctx='kubectl config use-context'
alias kns='kubectl config set-context --current --namespace'
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `KUBECONFIG` | Path to kubeconfig file(s), colon-separated (default: `~/.kube/config`) |
| `KUBE_CONTEXT` | Override active context without switching |
| `KUBECTL_EXTERNAL_DIFF` | Custom diff tool for `kubectl diff` (e.g., `dyff`) |
| `KUBE_EDITOR` | Editor for `kubectl edit` (default: `$EDITOR` or `vi`) |

sections.title: Context and kubeconfig management
sections.body: kubectl uses `~/.kube/config` (or `$KUBECONFIG`) to determine the target cluster, user credentials, and default namespace. A context combines a cluster + user + namespace. Use `kubectl config get-contexts` to list, `kubectl config use-context <name>` to switch, `kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=<ns>` to set default namespace. Merge multiple kubeconfigs by setting `KUBECONFIG=file1:file2` and running `kubectl config view --flatten`. Tools like `kubectx` and `kubens` provide faster switching.

sections.title: Output formatting and parsing
sections.body: kubectl supports multiple output formats: `-o wide` for extra columns, `-o json` and `-o yaml` for machine-readable output, `-o jsonpath='{.field}'` for extracting specific fields, `-o custom-columns=COL:path` for tabular extraction. JSONPath supports array indexing (`[0]`), wildcards (`[*]`), ranges, and filters. Combine with `--sort-by=.field` for sorted output. Use `jq` for complex JSON processing: `kubectl get pods -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'`.

sections.title: Dry run and manifest generation
sections.body: Use `--dry-run=client -o yaml` with any imperative command to generate a YAML manifest without creating the resource. This is the fastest way to scaffold manifests: `kubectl create deployment web --image=nginx --dry-run=client -o yaml > deploy.yaml`. Use `kubectl diff -f manifest.yaml` to preview changes before applying (server-side diff). Use `--dry-run=server` for full validation including admission controllers and webhooks.

sections.title: Debugging with kubectl
sections.body: Core debug commands: `kubectl logs <pod> --tail=100` for application output, `kubectl logs <pod> --previous` for crashed container logs, `kubectl exec -it <pod> -- /bin/sh` for interactive shell, `kubectl debug pod/<pod> -it --image=busybox` for ephemeral debug containers (k8s 1.23+), `kubectl port-forward pod/<pod> 8080:8080` for local access, `kubectl cp <pod>:/path ./local` for file retrieval. Run disposable debug pods with `kubectl run debug --rm -it --image=nicolaka/netshoot -- /bin/bash` for network and DNS testing. See [[kubernetes/troubleshooting]] for systematic workflows.

sections.title: Apply strategies and kustomize
sections.body: Three apply modes: `kubectl apply -f` for declarative management (recommended), `kubectl create` for imperative one-off creation, `kubectl replace` for full replacement. Apply supports directories (`-f ./k8s/`), URLs, stdin (`cat manifest.yaml | kubectl apply -f -`), and kustomize (`-k ./overlays/prod/`). Use `--prune -l app=name` to delete resources not in the current manifest set. Use `kubectl apply --server-side` for conflict detection in multi-team environments.
